message.content:
Search the sglang source tree at /sgl-workspace/sglang/python/sglang/ for how MoE (Mixture of Experts) execution is configured, which runners/backends are available, and how the backend is selected for different hardware (especially AMD/ROCm). Look for:
1. MoE runner selection logic (files like fused_moe*.py, moe*.py)
2. Any references to "rocm", "amd", "hip", "gfx950", "mi300", "mi350" in MoE code
3. How the model config for gemma-4-26B maps to MoE execution paths
4. Any fallback logic where a faster backend is skipped on AMD

Be very thorough - search across srt/layers/, srt/model_executor/, and any other relevant directories.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- dataviz: Use this skill whenever you are about to create ANY chart, graph, plot, dashboard, or data visualization, in ANY output medium — an HTML or React artifact, inline SVG, plotting code in any library (matplotlib, plotly, d3, Recharts, …), an image/PNG you will render and upload, or a chart shared into Slack. Read it BEFORE writing the first line of chart code, choosing chart colors, building a stat tile / meter / KPI row, or laying out a dashboard. Produces visualizations that read as one system — elegant, accessible, consistent in light and dark — using a brand-neutral placeholder palette you swap for your own. Teaches a design-system-agnostic method: a form heuristic, a color formula with a runnable validator, mark specs, and interaction rules. A validated default palette is documented in `references/palette.md` — swap that file's values for your brand's. Triggers on: "chart", "graph", "plot", "data viz", "visualization", "dashboard", "analytics", "visualize data", "categorical colors", "sequential / diverging palette", "stat tile", "sparkline", "heatmap", "legend", "axis", "tooltip", "chart colors", "color by series".
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by exercising it end-to-end and observing behavior — drive the affected flow, not just tests or typecheck. Run before committing nontrivial changes; bootstraps this repo's project verify skill if none exists yet. Don't invoke it on a diff that only touches tests, docs, or other code with no runtime surface to drive (a change to product source always has one) — there's nothing to observe.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo, defaults to 10m) - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

1	from __future__ import annotations
2	
3	import logging
4	import os
5	from typing import TYPE_CHECKING, Any, Optional
6	
7	from sglang.srt.layers.moe.moe_runner.base import (
8	    FusedOpPool,
9	    MoeRunnerConfig,
10	    PermuteMethodPool,
11	)
12	from sglang.srt.layers.moe.moe_runner.deep_gemm import DeepGemmRunnerCore
13	from sglang.srt.layers.moe.moe_runner.triton import TritonRunnerCore
14	from sglang.srt.layers.moe.moe_runner.triton_kernels import TritonKernelsRunnerCore
15	from sglang.srt.layers.moe.utils import get_moe_a2a_backend
16	
17	if TYPE_CHECKING:
18	    from sglang.srt.batch_overlap.single_batch_overlap import DownGemmOverlapArgs
19	    from sglang.srt.layers.moe.moe_runner.base import MoeQuantInfo
20	    from sglang.srt.layers.moe.token_dispatcher.base import CombineInput, DispatchOutput
21	    from sglang.srt.layers.moe.utils import MoeRunnerBackend
22	    from sglang.srt.lora.lora_moe_runners import LoRAHooks
23	
24	logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
25	
26	
27	class MoeRunner:
28	    def __init__(
29	        self,
30	        runner_backend: MoeRunnerBackend,
31	        config: MoeRunnerConfig,
32	        lora_enabled: bool = False,
33	    ):
34	        self.runner_backend = runner_backend
35	        self.config = config
36	        self.lora_enabled = lora_enabled
37	
38	        self.fused_func = None
39	
40	        if runner_backend.is_triton():
41	            self.runner_core = TritonRunnerCore(config)
42	        elif runner_backend.is_triton_kernels():
43	            self.runner_core = TritonKernelsRunnerCore(config)
44	        elif runner_backend.is_deep_gemm():
45	            self.runner_core = DeepGemmRunnerCore(config)
46	        elif runner_backend.is_aiter():
47	            from sglang.srt.layers.moe.moe_runner.aiter import AiterRunnerCore
48	
49	            self.runner_core = AiterRunnerCore(config)
50	        elif runner_backend.is_marlin():
51	            if lora_enabled:
52	                from sglang.srt.lora.lora_moe_runner_marlin import MarlinLoraRunnerCore
53	
54	                self.runner_core = MarlinLoraRunnerCore(config)
55	            else:
56	                self.runner_core = None  # Marlin only supports fused path
57	        elif (
58	            runner_backend.is_flashinfer_trtllm()
59	            or runner_backend.is_flashinfer_trtllm_routed()
60	        ):
61	            self.runner_core = None  # FlashInfer TRT-LLM only supports fused path
62	        elif runner_backend.is_flashinfer_cutedsl():
63	            self.runner_core = None  # FlashInfer CuteDSL only supports fused path
64	        elif runner_backend.is_flashinfer_cutlass():
65	            self.runner_core = None  # FlashInfer CUTLASS only supports fused path
66	        elif runner_backend.is_flashinfer_mxfp4():
67	            self.runner_core = None  # FlashInfer MXFP4 only supports fused path
68	        elif runner_backend.is_cutlass():
69	            self.runner_core = None  # CUTLASS uses the direct cutlass_moe_fp4 path
70	        else:
71	            raise NotImplementedError(f"Unsupported runner backend: {runner_backend}")
72	
73	        # Skip fused func if LoRA is enabled (LoRA requires non-fused path)
74	        if not lora_enabled:
75	            a2a_backend_name = get_moe_a2a_backend().value
76	            runner_backend_name = runner_backend.value
77	
78	            # TODO(cwan): add a server argument to disable fused func
79	            self.fused_func = FusedOpPool.get_fused_func(
80	                a2a_backend_name, runner_backend_name
81	            )
82	
83	            if self.runner_core is None and self.fused_func is None:
84	                raise NotImplementedError(
85	                    f"Runner backend {runner_backend} requires a fused func for a2a backend "
86	                    f"{a2a_backend_name}, but none is registered."
87	                )
88	
89	        self.down_gemm_overlap_args: Optional[DownGemmOverlapArgs] = None
90	        self.meta_overlap_args: Optional[dict] = None
91	
92	        SGLANG_CI_DISABLE_MOE_FUSED_FUNC = os.environ.get(
93	            "SGLANG_CI_DISABLE_MOE_FUSED_FUNC", "0"
94	        )
95	        if SGLANG_CI_DISABLE_MOE_FUSED_FUNC == "1":
96	            logger.info(
97	                "SGLANG_CI_DISABLE_MOE_FUSED_FUNC is set to 1, disabling fused func"
98	            )
99	            self.fused_func = None
100	
101	    def run(
102	        self, dispatch_output: DispatchOutput, quant_info: MoeQuantInfo, lora_info=None
103	    ) -> CombineInput:
104	        if self.fused_func is not None and not self.lora_enabled:
105	            return self.fused_func(dispatch_output, quant_info, self.config)
106	
107	        assert self.runner_core is not None
108	
109	        def _maybe_build_lora_hooks(_runner_input: Any) -> LoRAHooks:
110	            from sglang.srt.layers.moe.token_dispatcher.base import DispatchOutput
111	            from sglang.srt.lora.lora_moe_runners import build_lora_hooks
112	
113	            if isinstance(_runner_input, DispatchOutput):
114	                hidden_states, topk_ids = (
115	                    _runner_input.hidden_states,
116	                    _runner_input.topk_output.topk_ids,
117	                )
118	            else:
119	                hidden_states = _runner_input.hidden_states
120	                topk_ids = getattr(_runner_input, "topk_ids", None)
121	            if self.lora_enabled and lora_info is not None:
122	                return build_lora_hooks(
123	                    hidden_states,
124	                    lora_info,
125	                    topk_ids,
126	                )
127	            return None
128	
129	        # Runners that handle dispatch_output directly (e.g., MarlinRunnerCore)
130	        # bypass the pre-permute step and do their own alignment internally.
131	        if hasattr(self.runner_core, "run_from_dispatch"):
132	            hooks = _maybe_build_lora_hooks(dispatch_output)
133	            return self.runner_core.run_from_dispatch(
134	                dispatch_output, quant_info, self.config, hooks=hooks
135	            )
136	
137	        dispatch_format = dispatch_output.format.value
138	        runner_format = self.runner_core.runner_backend.value
139	        self.pre_permute_func = PermuteMethodPool.get_pre_permute(
140	            dispatch_format, runner_format
141	        )
142	
143	        running_state = {}
144	        if self.down_gemm_overlap_args is not None:
145	            running_state["down_gemm_overlap_args"] = self.down_gemm_overlap_args
146	        if self.meta_overlap_args is not None:
147	            running_state["meta_overlap_args"] = self.meta_overlap_args
148	
149	        runner_input = self.pre_permute_func(
150	            dispatch_output, quant_info, self.config, running_state
151	        )
152	
153	        hooks = _maybe_build_lora_hooks(runner_input)
154	
155	        runner_output = self.runner_core.run(
156	            runner_input, quant_info, running_state, hooks=hooks
157	        )
158	        runner_format = self.runner_core.runner_backend.value
159	        combine_format = dispatch_output.format.value
160	        self.post_permute_func = PermuteMethodPool.get_post_permute(
161	            runner_format, combine_format
162	        )
163	        combine_input = self.post_permute_func(
164	            runner_output, quant_info, self.config, running_state
165	        )
166	
167	        return combine_input
168	
169	    def set_overlap_args(
170	        self, down_gemm_overlap_args: DownGemmOverlapArgs, meta_overlap_args: dict
171	    ):
172	        self.down_gemm_overlap_args = down_gemm_overlap_args
173	        self.meta_overlap_args = meta_overlap_args
174	
175	    def clear_overlap_args(self) -> None:
176	        self.down_gemm_overlap_args = None
177	        self.meta_overlap_args = None
178

1	# Adapted from https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm/blob/v0.9.1rc2/vllm/model_executor/layers/fused_moe/rocm_aiter_fused_moe.py
2	# SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0
3	# SPDX-FileCopyrightText: Copyright contributors to the vLLM project
4	from enum import IntEnum
5	from typing import Optional
6	
7	import torch
8	import triton
9	import triton.language as tl
10	
11	from sglang.srt.utils import get_bool_env_var, is_hip
12	from sglang.srt.utils.custom_op import register_custom_op
13	
14	_is_hip = is_hip()
15	_use_aiter = get_bool_env_var("SGLANG_USE_AITER") and _is_hip
16	
17	
18	class ActivationMethod(IntEnum):
19	    # This allows interfacing with AITER ActivationType enum
20	    # without importing the ActivationType enum from AITER globally.
21	    SILU = 0
22	    GELU = 1
23	
24	
25	# NOTE: for non _use_aiter case, use lazy registration to avoid overhead
26	# (registration may not be trigger actually, since it will not be called)
27	@register_custom_op(out_shape="hidden_states", eager=_use_aiter)
28	def rocm_aiter_asm_moe_tkw1(
29	    hidden_states: torch.Tensor,
30	    w1: torch.Tensor,
31	    w2: torch.Tensor,
32	    topk_weights: torch.Tensor,
33	    topk_ids: torch.Tensor,
34	    fc1_scale: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None,
35	    fc2_scale: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None,
36	    fc1_smooth_scale: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None,
37	    fc2_smooth_scale: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None,
38	    a16: bool = False,
39	    per_tensor_quant_scale: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None,
40	    expert_mask: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None,
41	    activation_method: int = ActivationMethod.SILU.value,
42	) -> torch.Tensor:
43	
44	    from aiter import ActivationType
45	    from aiter.fused_moe_bf16_asm import asm_moe_tkw1
46	
47	    activation = ActivationType(activation_method)
48	
49	    return asm_moe_tkw1(
50	        hidden_states,
51	        w1,
52	        w2,
53	        topk_weights,
54	        topk_ids,
55	        fc1_scale=fc1_scale,
56	        fc2_scale=fc2_scale,
57	        fc1_smooth_scale=fc1_smooth_scale,
58	        fc2_smooth_scale=fc2_smooth_scale,
59	        a16=a16,
60	        per_tensor_quant_scale=per_tensor_quant_scale,
61	        expert_mask=expert_mask,
62	        activation=activation,
63	    )
64	
65	
66	def rocm_fused_experts_tkw1(
67	    hidden_states: torch.Tensor,
68	    w1: torch.Tensor,
69	    w2: torch.Tensor,
70	    topk_weights: torch.Tensor,
71	    topk_ids: torch.Tensor,
72	    activation: str = "silu",
73	    apply_router_weight_on_input: bool = False,
74	    use_fp8_w8a8: bool = False,
75	    per_channel_quant: bool = False,
76	    w1_scale: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None,
77	    w2_scale: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None,
78	    a1_scale: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None,
79	    a2_scale: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None,
80	    block_shape: Optional[list[int]] = None,
81	) -> torch.Tensor:
82	
83	    activation_method = (
84	        ActivationMethod.SILU if activation == "silu" else ActivationMethod.GELU
85	    )
86	    # All AITER Fused MoE kernels are expecting the following datatypes
87	    topk_weights = topk_weights.to(torch.float32)
88	    topk_ids = topk_ids.to(torch.int32)
89	
90	    # w8a8 per-channel quantization
91	    if per_channel_quant and apply_router_weight_on_input and use_fp8_w8a8:
92	        # AITER tkw1 kernel for FP8 models with `apply_router_weight_on_input`
93	        # This applies topk_weights on the GEMM output of the first FC layer
94	        #  rather than the second FC.
95	        assert (
96	            topk_weights.dim() == 2
97	        ), "`topk_weights` should be in shape (num_tokens, topk)"
98	        assert topk_weights.shape[-1] == 1, (
99	            "Only support topk=1 when" " `apply_router_weight_on_input` is True"
100	        )
101	
102	        return rocm_aiter_asm_moe_tkw1(
103	            hidden_states,
104	            w1,
105	            w2,
106	            topk_weights,
107	            topk_ids,
108	            fc1_scale=w1_scale,
109	            fc2_scale=w2_scale,
110	            fc1_smooth_scale=None,
111	            fc2_smooth_scale=None,
112	            a16=False,
113	            per_tensor_quant_scale=None,
114	            expert_mask=None,
115	            activation_method=activation_method,
116	        )
117	    else:
118	        assert False, "This should not be called."
119	
120	
121	@triton.jit
122	def upscale_kernel(
123	    A_ptr,  # *fp16 / *fp32
124	    scale_ptr,  # *fp16 / *fp32
125	    Out_ptr,  # *fp16 / *fp32
126	    M,
127	    N,
128	    recv_token_num,
129	    stride_am,
130	    stride_an,
131	    stride_sm,
132	    stride_sn,
133	    stride_om,
134	    stride_on,
135	    BLOCK_N: tl.constexpr,
136	):
137	    pid_m = tl.program_id(0)  # row id
138	    pid_n = tl.program_id(1)  # block id along N
139	
140	    recv_token_num_val = tl.load(recv_token_num)
141	
142	    if pid_m >= recv_token_num_val:
143	        return
144	
145	    # column offsets
146	    offs_n = pid_n * BLOCK_N + tl.arange(0, BLOCK_N)
147	    mask = offs_n < N
148	
149	    # A[m, n]
150	    a_ptrs = A_ptr + pid_m * stride_am + offs_n * stride_an
151	    a = tl.load(a_ptrs, mask=mask, other=0.0)
152	
153	    # scale index: n // 128
154	    scale_idx = offs_n // 128
155	    s_ptrs = scale_ptr + pid_m * stride_sm + scale_idx * stride_sn
156	    s = tl.load(s_ptrs, mask=mask, other=1.0)
157	
158	    out = a * s
159	
160	    out_ptrs = Out_ptr + pid_m * stride_om + offs_n * stride_on
161	    tl.store(out_ptrs, out, mask=mask)
162	
163	
164	def upscale(hidden_state, hidden_state_scale, recv_token_num, output_dtype):
165	    M, N = hidden_state.shape
166	
167	    Out = torch.empty_like(hidden_state, dtype=output_dtype)
168	
169	    BLOCK_N = 256
170	
171	    grid = (M, triton.cdiv(N, BLOCK_N))
172	
173	    upscale_kernel[grid](
174	        hidden_state,
175	        hidden_state_scale,
176	        Out,
177	        M,
178	        N,
179	        recv_token_num,
180	        hidden_state.stride(0),
181	        hidden_state.stride(1),
182	        hidden_state_scale.stride(0),
183	        hidden_state_scale.stride(1),
184	        Out.stride(0),
185	        Out.stride(1),
186	        BLOCK_N=BLOCK_N,
187	    )
188	
189	    return Out
190	
191	
192	@triton.jit
193	def upscale_fp4x2_block32_kernel(
194	    A_u8_ptr,  # *uint8  (view from float4_e2m1fn_x2)
195	    S_u8_ptr,  # *uint8  (view from float8_e8m0fnu), shape (M, N_fp4/32)
196	    Out_ptr,  # *fp16/fp32/bf16, shape (M, N_fp4)
197	    N_FP4: tl.constexpr,
198	    recv_token_num,
199	    stride_am,
200	    stride_an,  # A strides (in uint8 elements) for (M, packed_N)
201	    stride_sm,
202	    stride_sn,  # S strides (in uint8 elements) for (M, N_FP4/32)
203	    stride_om,
204	    stride_on,  # Out strides (in output elements) for (M, N_FP4)
205	    BLOCK_N: tl.constexpr,
206	    OUT_DTYPE: tl.constexpr,  # tl.float16 / tl.float32 / tl.bfloat16
207	):
208	    pid_m = tl.program_id(0)
209	    pid_n = tl.program_id(1)
210	
211	    recv_token_num_val = tl.load(recv_token_num)
212	    if pid_m >= recv_token_num_val:
213	        return
214	
215	    offs = pid_n * BLOCK_N + tl.arange(0, BLOCK_N)
216	    mask = offs < N_FP4
217	
218	    # --------------------------
219	    # Load packed fp4x2 byte
220	    # --------------------------
221	    byte_idx = offs >> 1  # offs // 2
222	    is_hi = (offs & 1) != 0  # select high nibble?
223	
224	    a_ptrs = A_u8_ptr + pid_m * stride_am + byte_idx * stride_an
225	    a_byte = tl.load(a_ptrs, mask=mask, other=0).to(tl.int32)
226	
227	    lo = a_byte & 0xF
228	    hi = (a_byte >> 4) & 0xF
229	    code = tl.where(is_hi, hi, lo).to(tl.int32)  # 0..15
230	
231	    # --------------------------
232	    # Decode float4_e2m1fn
233	    # layout: [sign|exp(2)|mant(1)]
234	    # bias=1, finite-only
235	    # --------------------------
236	    sign = (code >> 3) & 0x1
237	    exp = (code >> 1) & 0x3
238	    mant = code & 0x1
239	
240	    mant_f = mant.to(tl.float32) * 0.5
241	    is_sub = exp == 0
242	
243	    # normal: 2^(exp-bias) * (1 + mant/2), bias=1
244	    e_norm = (exp - 1).to(tl.float32)
245	    val_norm = tl.exp2(e_norm) * (1.0 + mant_f)
246	
247	    # subnorm/zero: mant/2 * 2^(1-bias) = mant/2
248	    val_sub = mant_f
249	
250	    val = tl.where(is_sub, val_sub, val_norm)
251	    val = tl.where(sign != 0, -val, val)  # apply sign
252	
253	    # --------------------------
254	    # Per-token block32 scale: scale_idx = offs // 32
255	    # scale dtype: float8_e8m0fnu stored in uint8
256	    # decode: e==0 -> 0
257	    #         e in [1..254] -> 2^(e-127)
258	    #         e==255 -> clamp to 254
259	    # --------------------------
260	    scale_idx = offs >> 5  # offs // 32
261	
262	    s_ptrs = S_u8_ptr + pid_m * stride_sm + scale_idx * stride_sn
263	    e = tl.load(s_ptrs, mask=mask, other=0).to(tl.int32)
264	
265	    e = tl.minimum(e, 254)  # clamp 255->254
266	    is_zero = e == 0
267	    exp_s = (e - 127).to(tl.float32)
268	    s = tl.exp2(exp_s)
269	    s = tl.where(is_zero, 0.0, s)
270	
271	    out = (val * s).to(OUT_DTYPE)
272	
273	    out_ptrs = Out_ptr + pid_m * stride_om + offs * stride_on
274	    tl.store(out_ptrs, out, mask=mask)
275	
276	
277	def upscale_mxfp4(hidden_state, hidden_state_scale, recv_token_num, output_dtype):
278	    """
279	    hidden_state: (M, packed_N) torch.float4_e2m1fn_x2
280	    hidden_state_scale: (M, packed_N*2/32) = (M, N_fp4/32) torch.float8_e8m0fnu
281	    output: (M, N_fp4) output_dtype
282	    """
283	    assert hidden_state.dtype == torch.float4_e2m1fn_x2, hidden_state.dtype
284	    assert hidden_state_scale.dtype == torch.float8_e8m0fnu, hidden_state_scale.dtype
285	    assert hidden_state.is_contiguous() or True  # stride-based load OK
286	
287	    M, packed_N = hidden_state.shape
288	    N_fp4 = packed_N * 2
289	
290	    # scale second dim must be N_fp4/32
291	    assert hidden_state_scale.shape[0] == M
292	    assert hidden_state_scale.shape[1] == (N_fp4 // 32), (
293	        hidden_state_scale.shape,
294	        N_fp4,
295	    )
296	
297	    # Triton doesn't (reliably) accept torch.float4/float8 pointers directly.
298	    # Use raw uint8 views.
299	    A_u8 = hidden_state.view(torch.uint8)
300	    S_u8 = hidden_state_scale.view(torch.uint8)
301	
302	    Out = torch.empty((M, N_fp4), dtype=output_dtype, device=hidden_state.device)
303	
304	    BLOCK_N = 256
305	    grid = (M, triton.cdiv(N_fp4, BLOCK_N))
306	
307	    OUT_TL = (
308	        tl.float16
309	        if output_dtype == torch.float16
310	        else tl.bfloat16 if output_dtype == torch.bfloat16 else tl.float32
311	    )
312	
313	    upscale_fp4x2_block32_kernel[grid](
314	        A_u8,
315	        S_u8,
316	        Out,
317	        N_FP4=N_fp4,
318	        recv_token_num=recv_token_num,
319	        stride_am=A_u8.stride(0),
320	        stride_an=A_u8.stride(1),
321	        stride_sm=S_u8.stride(0),
322	        stride_sn=S_u8.stride(1),
323	        stride_om=Out.stride(0),
324	        stride_on=Out.stride(1),
325	        BLOCK_N=BLOCK_N,
326	        OUT_DTYPE=OUT_TL,
327	        num_warps=4,
328	    )
329	    return Out
330

122	class FusedOpPool:
123	    _fused_funcs: dict[str, Callable] = {}
124	
125	    @classmethod
126	    def register_fused_func(
127	        cls, a2a_backend_name: str, runner_backend_name: str, fused_func: Callable
128	    ):
129	        key = (a2a_backend_name, runner_backend_name)
130	        if key in cls._fused_funcs:
131	            raise ValueError(
132	                f"Fused function for {a2a_backend_name} to {runner_backend_name} is already registered."
133	            )
134	        assert MoeA2ABackend(
135	            a2a_backend_name
136	        ), f"Invalid dispatch name: {a2a_backend_name}"
137	        assert MoeRunnerBackend(
138	            runner_backend_name
139	        ), f"Invalid runner name: {runner_backend_name}"
140	        cls._fused_funcs[key] = fused_func
141	
142	    @classmethod
143	    def get_fused_func(cls, dispatch_name: str, runner_name: str) -> Optional[Callable]:
144	        key = (dispatch_name, runner_name)
145	        fused_func = cls._fused_funcs.get(key)
146	        return fused_func

149	class PermuteMethodPool:
150	    _pre_permute_methods: dict[
151	        Tuple[DispatchOutputFormat, MoeRunnerBackend], Callable
152	    ] = {}
153	    _post_permute_methods: dict[
154	        Tuple[MoeRunnerBackend, CombineInputFormat], Callable
155	    ] = {}
156	
157	    @classmethod
158	    def register_pre_permute(
159	        cls,
160	        dispatch_output_name: str,
161	        runner_backend_name: str,
162	        permute_func: Callable,
163	    ):
164	        """
165	        Register a customized pre-permute function for the given DispatchOutputFormat and MoeRunnerBackend.
166	
167	        :param dispatch_output_name: The DispatchOutputFormat name.
168	        :param runner_backend_name: The MoeRunnerBackend name.
169	        :param permute_func: The permute function to register.
170	        """
171	        # TODO: check if registration is valid
172	        key = (dispatch_output_name, runner_backend_name)
173	        if key in cls._pre_permute_methods:
174	            raise ValueError(
175	                f"Pre-permute method for {dispatch_output_name} to {runner_backend_name} is already registered."
176	            )
177	        cls._pre_permute_methods[key] = permute_func
178	
179	    @classmethod
180	    def register_post_permute(
181	        cls,
182	        runner_backend_name: str,
183	        combine_input_name: str,
184	        permute_func: Callable,
185	    ):
186	        """
187	        Register a customized post-permute function for the given MoeRunnerBackend and CombineInputFormat.
188	
189	        :param runner_backend_name: The MoeRunnerBackend name.
190	        :param combine_input_name: The CombineInputFormat name.
191	        :param permute_func: The permute function to register.
192	        """
193	        # TODO: check if registration is valid
194	        key = (runner_backend_name, combine_input_name)
195	        if key in cls._post_permute_methods:
196	            raise ValueError(
197	                f"Post-permute method for {runner_backend_name} to {combine_input_name} is already registered."
198	            )
199	        cls._post_permute_methods[key] = permute_func
200	
201	    @classmethod
202	    def get_pre_permute(
203	        cls,
204	        dispatch_output_format: DispatchOutputFormat,
205	        runner_input_format: MoeRunnerBackend,
206	    ) -> Callable:
207	        """
208	        Retrieve the pre-permute function for the given DispatchOutputFormat and MoeRunnerBackend.
209	
210	        :param dispatch_output_format: The DispatchOutputFormat type.
211	        :param runner_input_format: The MoeRunnerBackend type.
212	        :return: The registered permute function or None if not found.
213	        """
214	        key = (dispatch_output_format, runner_input_format)
215	        pre_permute_func = cls._pre_permute_methods.get(key)
216	        assert (
217	            pre_permute_func is not None
218	        ), f"Pre-permute function for {dispatch_output_format} to {runner_input_format} is not registered"
219	        return pre_permute_func
220	
221	    @classmethod
222	    def get_post_permute(
223	        cls,
224	        runner_output_format: MoeRunnerBackend,
225	        combine_input_format: CombineInputFormat,
226	    ) -> Callable:
227	        """
228	        Retrieve the post-permute function for the given MoeRunnerBackend and CombineInputFormat.
229	
230	        :param runner_output_format: The MoeRunnerBackend type.
231	        :param combine_input_format: The CombineInputFormat type.
232	        :return: The registered permute function or None if not found.
233	        """
234	        key = (runner_output_format, combine_input_format)
235	        post_permute_func = cls._post_permute_methods.get(key)
236	        assert (
237	            post_permute_func is not None
238	        ), f"Post-permute function for {runner_output_format} to {combine_input_format} is not registered"
239	        return post_permute_func
240	
241	
242	def register_fused_func(
243	    a2a_backend_name: str,
244	    runner_backend_name: str,
245	) -> Callable:
246	    """
247	    Decorator to register a fused function for the given DispatchOutputFormat and MoeRunnerBackend.
248	
249	    :param a2a_backend_name: The A2A backend name.
250	    :param runner_backend_name: The MoeRunnerBackend name.
251	    :return: The decorator function.
252	    """
253	
254	    def decorator(fused_func: Callable):
255	        FusedOpPool.register_fused_func(
256	            a2a_backend_name, runner_backend_name, fused_func
257	        )
258	        return fused_func
259	
260	    return decorator
261	
262	
263	def register_pre_permute(
264	    dispatch_output_name: str,

268	    Decorator to register a pre-permute function for the given DispatchOutputFormat and MoeRunnerBackend.

270	    :param dispatch_output_name: The DispatchOutputFormat name.
271	    :param runner_backend_name: The MoeRunnerBackend name.
272	    :return: The decorator function.
273	    """
274	
275	    def decorator(
276	        permute_func: Callable[
277	            [DispatchOutput, MoeQuantInfo, MoeRunnerConfig, dict], RunnerInput
278	        ],

287	
288	def register_post_permute(
289	    runner_backend_name: str,
290	    combine_input_name: str,
291	) -> Callable:
292	    """
293	    Decorator to register a post-permute function for the given MoeRunnerBackend and CombineInputFormat.
294	
295	    :param runner_backend_name: The MoeRunnerBackend name.
296	    :param combine_input_name: The CombineInputFormat name.
297	    :return: The decorator function.
298	    """
299	
300	    def decorator(
301	        permute_func: Callable[
302	            [RunnerOutput, MoeQuantInfo, MoeRunnerConfig, dict], CombineInput
303	        ],
304	    ) -> Callable:
305	        PermuteMethodPool.register_post_permute(

1	"""Native MXFP8 (1x32 block, E8M0 scale) MoE for AMD CDNA4 (gfx950).
2	
3	Replaces the prior SGLang MXFP8 MoE family (dense / hybrid / packed /
4	grouped_gemm1 / grouped_gemm12 / compact / fused_act) with a single grouped
5	``tl.dot_scaled`` kernel. Instead of an explicit ``argsort`` + ``index_select``
6	gather, a materialized intermediate, a separate activation quant, and a
7	``tl.atomic_add`` combine:
8	
9	  * tokens are sorted by expert with ``moe_align_block_size``;
10	  * GEMM1 reads the activation by token-id indirection (``a_row = token // top_k``)
11	    so the hidden states are MXFP8-quantized exactly ONCE (not top_k times);
12	  * the SwiGLU-OAI activation is the fused fp32 Triton kernel (split layout);
13	  * GEMM2 applies the top-k weight inside the kernel and writes each route to a
14	    distinct output row (no atomics); the final reduction is a strided sum.
15	"""
16	
17	from __future__ import annotations
18	
19	from typing import Optional
20	
21	import torch
22	import triton
23	import triton.language as tl
24	
25	from sglang.srt.environ import envs
26	from sglang.srt.layers.moe.moe_runner.triton_utils.moe_align_block_size import (
27	    moe_align_block_size,
28	)
29	from sglang.srt.layers.quantization.mxfp8_amd_gfx95 import mxfp8_e4m3_quantize
30	
31	
32	@triton.jit
33	def _mxfp8_grouped_gemm_kernel(
34	    a_ptr,
35	    a_scale_ptr,
36	    b_ptr,
37	    b_scale_ptr,
38	    c_ptr,
39	    topk_weights_ptr,
40	    sorted_token_ids_ptr,
41	    expert_ids_ptr,
42	    num_tokens_post_padded_ptr,
43	    E,
44	    N,
45	    K,
46	    num_valid_tokens,
47	    top_k,
48	    stride_am,
49	    stride_ak,
50	    stride_asm,
51	    stride_ask,
52	    stride_be,
53	    stride_bn,
54	    stride_bk,
55	    stride_bse,
56	    stride_bsn,
57	    stride_bsk,
58	    stride_cm,
59	    stride_cn,
60	    A_DIV: tl.constexpr,
61	    MUL_WEIGHT: tl.constexpr,
62	    BLOCK_M: tl.constexpr,
63	    BLOCK_N: tl.constexpr,
64	    BLOCK_K: tl.constexpr,
65	):
66	    pid_m = tl.program_id(0)
67	    pid_n = tl.program_id(1)
68	    num_post = tl.load(num_tokens_post_padded_ptr)
69	    if pid_m * BLOCK_M >= num_post:
70	        return
71	
72	    offs_tid = pid_m * BLOCK_M + tl.arange(0, BLOCK_M)
73	    offs_token = tl.load(sorted_token_ids_ptr + offs_tid).to(tl.int64)
74	    token_mask = offs_token < num_valid_tokens
75	    off_e = tl.load(expert_ids_ptr + pid_m).to(tl.int64)
76	    valid_expert = (off_e >= 0) & (off_e < E)
77	
78	    offs_n = pid_n * BLOCK_N + tl.arange(0, BLOCK_N)
79	    offs_k = tl.arange(0, BLOCK_K)
80	    offs_sk = tl.arange(0, BLOCK_K // 32)
81	    a_row = offs_token // A_DIV
82	
83	    a_ptrs = a_ptr + a_row[:, None] * stride_am + offs_k[None, :] * stride_ak
84	    as_ptrs = a_scale_ptr + a_row[:, None] * stride_asm + offs_sk[None, :] * stride_ask
85	    b_ptrs = (
86	        b_ptr
87	        + off_e * stride_be
88	        + offs_n[:, None] * stride_bn
89	        + offs_k[None, :] * stride_bk
90	    )
91	    bs_ptrs = (
92	        b_scale_ptr
93	        + off_e * stride_bse
94	        + offs_n[:, None] * stride_bsn
95	        + offs_sk[None, :] * stride_bsk
96	    )
97	
98	    acc = tl.zeros((BLOCK_M, BLOCK_N), dtype=tl.float32)
99	    n_mask = offs_n < N
100	    for _ in range(0, tl.cdiv(K, BLOCK_K)):
101	        a = tl.load(a_ptrs, mask=token_mask[:, None], other=0.0)
102	        b = tl.load(b_ptrs, mask=valid_expert & n_mask[:, None], other=0.0)
103	        asc = tl.load(as_ptrs, mask=token_mask[:, None], other=0)
104	        bsc = tl.load(bs_ptrs, mask=valid_expert & n_mask[:, None], other=0)
105	        acc += tl.dot_scaled(a, asc, "e4m3", b.T, bsc, "e4m3")
106	
107	        a_ptrs += BLOCK_K * stride_ak
108	        b_ptrs += BLOCK_K * stride_bk
109	        as_ptrs += (BLOCK_K // 32) * stride_ask
110	        bs_ptrs += (BLOCK_K // 32) * stride_bsk
111	
112	    if MUL_WEIGHT:
113	        w = tl.load(topk_weights_ptr + offs_token, mask=token_mask, other=0.0)
114	        acc = acc * w[:, None]
115	
116	    c_ptrs = c_ptr + offs_token[:, None] * stride_cm + offs_n[None, :] * stride_cn
117	    tl.store(
118	        c_ptrs,
119	        acc.to(c_ptr.dtype.element_ty),
120	        mask=token_mask[:, None] & n_mask[None, :],
121	    )
122	
123	
124	def _grouped_gemm_mxfp8(
125	    a_q: torch.Tensor,  # [M, K] fp8 e4m3
126	    a_scale: torch.Tensor,  # [M, K//32] uint8 (E8M0)
127	    w: torch.Tensor,  # [E, N, K] fp8 e4m3
128	    w_scale: torch.Tensor,  # [E, N, K//32] uint8 (E8M0)
129	    sorted_token_ids: torch.Tensor,
130	    expert_ids: torch.Tensor,
131	    num_tokens_post_padded: torch.Tensor,
132	    num_valid_tokens: int,
133	    top_k: int,
134	    block_m: int,
135	    out_dtype: torch.dtype,
136	    a_div: int,
137	    mul_weight_by: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None,
138	) -> torch.Tensor:
139	    M_routed = num_valid_tokens
140	    E, N, K = w.shape
141	    assert K % 128 == 0, f"MXFP8 native MoE requires K%128==0, got K={K}"
142	    # Keep zero-fill: moe_align_block_size reserves an extra expert bucket for
143	    # filtered routes, which should contribute zeros if present.
144	    out = torch.zeros((M_routed, N), dtype=out_dtype, device=a_q.device)
145	    if a_div == top_k and M_routed <= 32 and K >= 3072:
146	        BLOCK_N = 64
147	        num_warps = 4
148	    else:
149	        BLOCK_N = 128
150	        num_warps = 8
151	    BLOCK_K = 128
152	    grid = (triton.cdiv(sorted_token_ids.shape[0], block_m), triton.cdiv(N, BLOCK_N))
153	    _mxfp8_grouped_gemm_kernel[grid](
154	        a_q,
155	        a_scale,
156	        w,
157	        w_scale,
158	        out,
159	        mul_weight_by if mul_weight_by is not None else a_q,
160	        sorted_token_ids,
161	        expert_ids,
162	        num_tokens_post_padded,
163	        E,
164	        N,
165	        K,
166	        num_valid_tokens,
167	        top_k,
168	        a_q.stride(0),
169	        a_q.stride(1),
170	        a_scale.stride(0),
171	        a_scale.stride(1),
172	        w.stride(0),
173	        w.stride(1),
174	        w.stride(2),
175	        w_scale.stride(0),
176	        w_scale.stride(1),
177	        w_scale.stride(2),
178	        out.stride(0),
179	        out.stride(1),
180	        A_DIV=a_div,
181	        MUL_WEIGHT=mul_weight_by is not None,
182	        BLOCK_M=block_m,
183	        BLOCK_N=BLOCK_N,
184	        BLOCK_K=BLOCK_K,
185	        num_warps=num_warps,
186	    )
187	    return out
188	
189	
190	@triton.jit
191	def _combine_topk_routes_kernel(
192	    route_ptr,
193	    out_ptr,
194	    H,
195	    top_k: tl.constexpr,
196	    stride_rm,
197	    stride_rh,
198	    stride_ot,
199	    stride_oh,
200	    BLOCK_H: tl.constexpr,
201	):
202	    token_id = tl.program_id(0)
203	    h_block = tl.program_id(1)
204	    offs_h = h_block * BLOCK_H + tl.arange(0, BLOCK_H)
205	    h_mask = offs_h < H
206	
207	    acc = tl.zeros((BLOCK_H,), dtype=tl.float32)
208	    for route_idx in range(0, top_k):
209	        route_row = token_id * top_k + route_idx
210	        vals = tl.load(
211	            route_ptr + route_row * stride_rm + offs_h * stride_rh,
212	            mask=h_mask,
213	            other=0.0,
214	        )
215	        acc += vals.to(tl.float32)
216	
217	    tl.store(out_ptr + token_id * stride_ot + offs_h * stride_oh, acc, mask=h_mask)
218	
219	
220	def _combine_topk_routes(
221	    route_outputs: torch.Tensor,
222	    num_tokens: int,
223	    top_k: int,
224	    hidden_size: int,
225	    out_dtype: torch.dtype,
226	) -> torch.Tensor:
227	    out = torch.empty(
228	        (num_tokens, hidden_size), dtype=out_dtype, device=route_outputs.device
229	    )
230	    block_h = 1024
231	    grid = (num_tokens, triton.cdiv(hidden_size, block_h))
232	    _combine_topk_routes_kernel[grid](
233	        route_outputs,
234	        out,
235	        hidden_size,
236	        top_k,
237	        route_outputs.stride(0),
238	        route_outputs.stride(1),
239	        out.stride(0),
240	        out.stride(1),
241	        BLOCK_H=block_h,
242	        num_warps=8,
243	    )
244	    return out
245	
246	
247	def fused_moe_mxfp8_native(
248	    hidden_states: torch.Tensor,  # [T, H] bf16
249	    w13: torch.Tensor,  # [E, 2I, H] fp8
250	    w13_scale: torch.Tensor,  # [E, 2I, H//32] uint8
251	    w2: torch.Tensor,  # [E, H, I] fp8
252	    w2_scale: torch.Tensor,  # [E, H, I//32] uint8
253	    topk_weights: torch.Tensor,  # [T, top_k]
254	    topk_ids: torch.Tensor,  # [T, top_k] (local expert ids; -1 for non-local EP)
255	    *,
256	    alpha: float,
257	    beta: float,
258	    limit: Optional[float],
259	    no_combine: bool = False,
260	    expert_map: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None,
261	) -> torch.Tensor:
262	    # Lazy import: the jit_kernel package pulls in Triton at first use; importing
263	    # at call time avoids any import-time cycle with the moe runner package.
264	    from sglang.jit_kernel.minimax_m3 import swiglu_oai_mxfp8_quant, swiglu_oai_split
265	
266	    T, H = hidden_states.shape
267	    top_k = topk_ids.shape[1]
268	    M = T * top_k
269	    local_num_experts = w13.shape[0]
270	
271	    if expert_map is not None:
272	        valid_global = (topk_ids >= 0) & (topk_ids < expert_map.numel())
273	        topk_ids = expert_map[topk_ids.clamp(0, expert_map.numel() - 1).long()].to(
274	            torch.int32
275	        )
276	        topk_ids.masked_fill_(
277	            ~valid_global | (topk_ids < 0) | (topk_ids >= local_num_experts), -1
278	        )
279	    else:
280	        topk_ids = topk_ids.to(torch.int32, copy=True)
281	        topk_ids.masked_fill_((topk_ids < 0) | (topk_ids >= local_num_experts), -1)
282	
283	    block_m = 64
284	    sorted_ids, expert_ids, num_post = moe_align_block_size(
285	        topk_ids, block_m, local_num_experts
286	    )
287	
288	    # GEMM1: x (mxfp8) @ w13^T -> [M, 2I]. The activation is quantized ONCE over
289	    # the T hidden rows; the kernel gathers per route via a_row = token // top_k.
290	    a_q, a_s = mxfp8_e4m3_quantize(hidden_states)
291	    g1 = _grouped_gemm_mxfp8(
292	        a_q,
293	        a_s,
294	        w13,
295	        w13_scale,
296	        sorted_ids,
297	        expert_ids,
298	        num_post,
299	        M,
300	        top_k,
301	        block_m,
302	        hidden_states.dtype,
303	        a_div=top_k,
304	    )  # [M, 2I]
305	
306	    if envs.SGLANG_MINIMAX_M3_FUSED_SWIGLU_MXFP8.get():
307	        # SwiGLU-OAI (split layout) + MiniMax MXFP8 quant in one kernel, fp32 all
308	        # the way to the E8M0 scale (no bf16 activation round-trip; matches the
309	        # vLLM/ame fused kernel). Opt-in until full-model accuracy is re-qualified.
310	        act_q, act_s = swiglu_oai_mxfp8_quant(g1, alpha=alpha, beta=beta, limit=limit)
311	    else:
312	        # Default accuracy path: keep the historical two-kernel boundary.
313	        act = swiglu_oai_split(
314	            g1, alpha=alpha, beta=beta, limit=limit, out_dtype=hidden_states.dtype
315	        )
316	        act_q, act_s = mxfp8_e4m3_quantize(act)
317	
318	    if no_combine:
319	        # Per-route outputs, unweighted, no reduction: [T, top_k, H].
320	        g2 = _grouped_gemm_mxfp8(
321	            act_q,
322	            act_s,
323	            w2,
324	            w2_scale,
325	            sorted_ids,
326	            expert_ids,
327	            num_post,
328	            M,
329	            top_k,
330	            block_m,
331	            hidden_states.dtype,
332	            a_div=1,
333	        )
334	        return g2.view(T, top_k, H)
335	
336	    # GEMM2: act (mxfp8) @ w2^T -> [M, H], weighted by topk_weights, then reduce.
337	    g2 = _grouped_gemm_mxfp8(
338	        act_q,
339	        act_s,
340	        w2,
341	        w2_scale,
342	        sorted_ids,
343	        expert_ids,
344	        num_post,
345	        M,
346	        top_k,
347	        block_m,
348	        torch.float32,
349	        a_div=1,
350	        mul_weight_by=topk_weights.reshape(-1).to(torch.float32),
351	    )  # [M, H] == [T*top_k, H]
352	
353	    if envs.SGLANG_MINIMAX_M3_FUSED_MOE_COMBINE.get():
354	        return _combine_topk_routes(g2, T, top_k, H, hidden_states.dtype)
355	    return g2.view(T, top_k, H).sum(dim=1).to(hidden_states.dtype)
356	
357	
358	def fused_experts_mxfp8(
359	    hidden_states: torch.Tensor,
360	    w1: torch.Tensor,
361	    w2: torch.Tensor,
362	    topk_weights: torch.Tensor,
363	    topk_ids: torch.Tensor,
364	    w1_scale: torch.Tensor,
365	    w2_scale: torch.Tensor,
366	    *,
367	    b1: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None,
368	    b2: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None,
369	    activation: str = "silu",
370	    is_gated: bool = True,
371	    no_combine: bool = False,
372	    inplace: bool = False,
373	    apply_router_weight_on_input: bool = False,
374	    routed_scaling_factor: Optional[float] = None,
375	    gemm1_alpha: Optional[float] = None,
376	    gemm1_limit: Optional[float] = None,
377	    swiglu_limit: Optional[float] = None,
378	    gate_up_interleaved: bool = True,
379	    expert_map: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None,
380	) -> torch.Tensor:
381	    """Native MXFP8 MoE entry (CDNA4 ``dot_scaled``).
382	
383	    Keeps the SGLang ``fused_experts_mxfp8`` call contract but routes to the
384	    single grouped-GEMM kernel. Only the MiniMax-M3 SwiGLU-OAI (split,
385	    uninterleaved, gated silu with ``gemm1_alpha``/``gemm1_limit``) configuration
386	    is supported -- the unsupported cases below never occur on the M3 path.
387	    """
388	    if not (activation == "silu" and is_gated):
389	        raise NotImplementedError(
390	            f"native MXFP8 MoE only supports gated swiglu-oai, got "
391	            f"{activation=} {is_gated=}."
392	        )
393	    if b1 is not None or b2 is not None:
394	        raise NotImplementedError("native MXFP8 MoE does not support expert bias.")
395	    if apply_router_weight_on_input:
396	        raise NotImplementedError(
397	            "native MXFP8 MoE does not support apply_router_weight_on_input."
398	        )
399	    if gate_up_interleaved:
400	        raise NotImplementedError(
401	            "native MXFP8 MoE expects uninterleaved (split) gate/up layout."
402	        )
403	
404	    # SwiGLU-OAI default activation alpha (gpt-oss); M3 may override via gemm1_alpha.
405	    alpha = 1.702 if gemm1_alpha is None else float(gemm1_alpha)
406	    beta = 1.0
407	    limit = None if gemm1_limit is None else float(gemm1_limit)
408	    # NOTE: routed_scaling_factor is intentionally NOT re-applied here. For M3
409	    # (sigmoid routing with apply_routed_scaling_factor_on_output=True) it is
410	    # already folded into topk_weights by the topk kernel; re-applying would
411	    # double-count it. This matches the prior SGLang MXFP8 behaviour.
412	
413	    out = fused_moe_mxfp8_native(
414	        hidden_states,
415	        w1,
416	        w1_scale,
417	        w2,
418	        w2_scale,
419	        topk_weights,
420	        topk_ids,
421	        alpha=alpha,
422	        beta=beta,
423	        limit=limit,
424	        no_combine=no_combine,
425	        expert_map=expert_map,
426	    )
427	
428	    if no_combine:
429	        return out
430	    if inplace:
431	        hidden_states.copy_(out)
432	        return hidden_states
433	    return out
434

1	from __future__ import annotations

21	    from sglang.srt.server_args import ServerArgs

23	from sglang.srt.server_args import get_global_server_args

28	class MoeA2ABackend(Enum):
29	
30	    NONE = "none"
31	    DEEPEP = "deepep"
32	    MOONCAKE = "mooncake"
33	    NIXL = "nixl"
34	    MORI = "mori"
35	    ASCEND_FUSEEP = "ascend_fuseep"
36	    FLASHINFER = "flashinfer"
37	    MEGAMOE = "megamoe"
38	    CUSTOMIZED = "customized"
39	
40	    @classmethod
41	    def _missing_(cls, value):
42	        if value is None:
43	            return cls.NONE
44	        for member in cls:
45	            if value == member.value:
46	                return member
47	        raise ValueError(f"No {cls.__name__} member for value {value}")
48	
49	    def is_none(self):
50	        return self == MoeA2ABackend.NONE
51	
52	    def is_deepep(self):
53	        return self == MoeA2ABackend.DEEPEP
54	
55	    def is_mooncake(self):
56	        return self == MoeA2ABackend.MOONCAKE
57	
58	    def is_nixl(self):
59	        return self == MoeA2ABackend.NIXL
60	
61	    def is_flashinfer(self):
62	        return self == MoeA2ABackend.FLASHINFER
63	
64	    def is_ascend_fuseep(self):
65	        return self == MoeA2ABackend.ASCEND_FUSEEP
66	
67	    def is_mori(self):
68	        return self == MoeA2ABackend.MORI
69	
70	    def is_megamoe(self):
71	        return self == MoeA2ABackend.MEGAMOE
72	
73	    def is_customized(self):
74	        return self == MoeA2ABackend.CUSTOMIZED
75	
76	    def supports_aiter(self) -> bool:
77	        return self in (
78	            MoeA2ABackend.NONE,
79	            MoeA2ABackend.DEEPEP,
80	            MoeA2ABackend.MOONCAKE,
81	            MoeA2ABackend.NIXL,
82	            MoeA2ABackend.MORI,
83	        )
84

86	class MoeRunnerBackend(Enum):
87	
88	    AUTO = "auto"
89	    DEEP_GEMM = "deep_gemm"
90	    TRITON = "triton"
91	    TRITON_KERNELS = "triton_kernel"
92	    FLASHINFER_TRTLLM = "flashinfer_trtllm"
93	    EXPERIMENTAL_SGL_TRTLLM = "experimental_sgl_trtllm"
94	    FLASHINFER_TRTLLM_ROUTED = "flashinfer_trtllm_routed"
95	    FLASHINFER_CUTLASS = "flashinfer_cutlass"
96	    FLASHINFER_MXFP4 = "flashinfer_mxfp4"
97	    FLASHINFER_CUTEDSL = "flashinfer_cutedsl"
98	    CUTLASS = "cutlass"
99	    MARLIN = "marlin"
100	    AITER = "aiter"
101	
102	    def is_auto(self):
103	        return self == MoeRunnerBackend.AUTO
104	
105	    def is_deep_gemm(self):
106	        return self == MoeRunnerBackend.DEEP_GEMM
107	
108	    def is_triton(self):
109	        return self == MoeRunnerBackend.TRITON

111	    def is_triton_kernels(self):
112	        return self == MoeRunnerBackend.TRITON_KERNELS

114	    def is_flashinfer_trtllm(self):
115	        # experimental_sgl_trtllm shares the TRT-LLM FP8 kernels + layout, so it inherits
116	        # trtllm weight-prep here; divergent sites check is_experimental_sgl_trtllm() first.
117	        return self in (
118	            MoeRunnerBackend.FLASHINFER_TRTLLM,
119	            MoeRunnerBackend.EXPERIMENTAL_SGL_TRTLLM,
120	        )
121	
122	    def is_experimental_sgl_trtllm(self):
123	        return self == MoeRunnerBackend.EXPERIMENTAL_SGL_TRTLLM
124	
125	    def is_flashinfer_trtllm_routed(self):
126	        return self == MoeRunnerBackend.FLASHINFER_TRTLLM_ROUTED
127	
128	    def is_flashinfer_cutlass(self):
129	        return self == MoeRunnerBackend.FLASHINFER_CUTLASS
130	
131	    def is_flashinfer_cutedsl(self):
132	        return self == MoeRunnerBackend.FLASHINFER_CUTEDSL
133	
134	    def is_flashinfer_mxfp4(self):
135	        return self == MoeRunnerBackend.FLASHINFER_MXFP4
136

138	        return self == MoeRunnerBackend.CUTLASS

142	
143	    def is_aiter(self):

146	
147	class DeepEPMode(Enum):
148	
149	    NORMAL = "normal"
150	    LOW_LATENCY = "low_latency"
151	    AUTO = "auto"
152	
153	    def enable_normal(self) -> bool:
154	        return self in [DeepEPMode.NORMAL, DeepEPMode.AUTO]
155	
156	    def enable_low_latency(self) -> bool:
157	        return self in [DeepEPMode.LOW_LATENCY, DeepEPMode.AUTO]
158	
159	    def resolve(self, is_extend_in_batch: bool) -> DeepEPMode:
160	        if self != DeepEPMode.AUTO:
161	            return self
162	
163	        if is_extend_in_batch:
164	            return DeepEPMode.NORMAL
165	        else:
166	            return DeepEPMode.LOW_LATENCY
167	
168	    def is_normal(self) -> bool:
169	        return self == DeepEPMode.NORMAL
170	
171	    def is_low_latency(self) -> bool:
172	        return self == DeepEPMode.LOW_LATENCY
173	
174	    def is_auto(self) -> bool:
175	        return self == DeepEPMode.AUTO
176	
177	
178	class DeepEPOutputDtype(Enum):
179	    """
180	    Describes the dispatch output data type for DeepEP.
181	
182	    - BF16: dispatch hidden states in bf16
183	    - FP8: dispatch hidden states in fp8
184	    - INT8: dispatch hidden states in int8
185	    - NVFP4: dispatch hidden states in nvfp4
186	    """
187	
188	    BF16 = "bf16"
189	    FP8 = "fp8"
190	    INT8 = "int8"
191	    NVFP4 = "nvfp4"
192	
193	
194	def get_deepep_output_dtype(self) -> DeepEPOutputDtype:
195	    """
196	    Automatically choose the dispatch output dtype for DeepEP.
197	
198	    The decision follows several checks in priority order:
199	    0. Parse server argument.
200	    1. Parse deprecated environment variables.
201	    2. If quant_config contains input_global_scale → NVFP4 path.
202	    3. Parse quant config
203	    4. If flashinfer_cutedsl or is_cutlass backend is active → BF16 (it quantizes hidden_states internally).
204	    5. Otherwise default for NPU → BF16 (the default for NPU).
205	    6. Otherwise → FP8 (the default for most models like DeepSeek-V3).
206	    """
207	
208	    # 0. Parse server argument.
209	    server_args = get_global_server_args()
210	    if server_args and server_args.deepep_dispatcher_output_dtype != "auto":
211	        return DeepEPOutputDtype(server_args.deepep_dispatcher_output_dtype)
212	
213	    # 1. Parse deprecated environment variables.
214	    if envs.SGLANG_DEEPEP_BF16_DISPATCH.get():
215	        logger.warning_once(
216	            "Warning: The env variable SGLANG_DEEPEP_BF16_DISPATCH deprecated "
217	            "and will be removed in future releases. Please use a new "
218	            "`--deepep-dispatcher-output-dtype bf16` argument instead."
219	        )
220	        return DeepEPOutputDtype.BF16
221	
222	    # 2. NVFP4 is detected inside dispatch_a / _dispatch_core via quant_config; no need to infer here.
223	    if self.quant_config is not None:
224	        input_global_scale = self.quant_config.get("input_global_scale", None)
225	        if input_global_scale is not None:
226	            return DeepEPOutputDtype.NVFP4
227	
228	        # 3. Parse quant config to determine the output dtype of dispatcher
229	        dispatcher_output_dtype = self.quant_config.get("dispatcher_output_dtype", None)
230	        if dispatcher_output_dtype is not None:
231	            return DeepEPOutputDtype(dispatcher_output_dtype)
232	
233	    # 4. flashinfer_cutedsl and is_cutlass expects BF16 dispatch
234	    if (
235	        get_moe_runner_backend().is_flashinfer_cutedsl()
236	        or get_moe_runner_backend().is_cutlass()
237	    ):
238	        return DeepEPOutputDtype.BF16
239	
240	    # 5. Default on NPU → BF16
241	    if _is_npu:
242	        return DeepEPOutputDtype.BF16
243	
244	    # 6. Default → FP8
245	    return DeepEPOutputDtype.FP8
246	
247	
248	MOE_A2A_BACKEND: Optional[MoeA2ABackend] = None
249	MOE_RUNNER_BACKEND: Optional[MoeRunnerBackend] = None
250	SPECULATIVE_MOE_RUNNER_BACKEND: Optional[MoeRunnerBackend] = None
251	SPECULATIVE_MOE_A2A_BACKEND: Optional[MoeA2ABackend] = None
252	DEEPEP_MODE: Optional[DeepEPMode] = None
253	IS_TBO_ENABLED: Optional[bool] = None
254	IS_SBO_ENABLED: Optional[bool] = None
255	TBO_TOKEN_DISTRIBUTION_THRESHOLD: Optional[float] = None
256	DEEPEP_CONFIG: Optional[str] = None
257	DISABLE_FLASHINFER_CUTLASS_MOE_FP4_ALLGATHER: Optional[bool] = None
258	MOE_QUANTIZATION: Optional[str] = None
259	
260	
261	def initialize_moe_config(server_args: ServerArgs):
262	    global MOE_A2A_BACKEND

411	def should_use_flashinfer_cutlass_moe_fp4_allgather():
412	    """
413	    Perform FP4 quantize before all-gather for flashinfer cutlass moe to reduce communication cost for high-throughput serving.
414	    """
415	    return (
416	        not DISABLE_FLASHINFER_CUTLASS_MOE_FP4_ALLGATHER
417	        and get_moe_a2a_backend().is_none()
418	        and get_moe_runner_backend().is_flashinfer_cutlass()
419	        and is_dp_attention_enabled()
420	        and MOE_QUANTIZATION == "modelopt_fp4"
421	        and get_parallel().moe_ep_size == get_parallel().attn_dp_size
422	    )

425	def should_use_dp_reduce_scatterv():
426	    """
427	    Use reduce_scatterv in the standard dispatcher's combine() for DP attention
428	    with EP, replacing the default all-reduce + dp_scatter path.
429	    Only changes the combine (post-kernel) communication; dispatch is unchanged.
430	    """
431	    return (
432	        not should_use_flashinfer_cutlass_moe_fp4_allgather()
433	        and get_moe_a2a_backend().is_none()
434	        and is_dp_attention_enabled()
435	        and get_parallel().attn_dp_size > 1
436	        and get_parallel().moe_ep_size == get_parallel().attn_dp_size
437	    )

440	def should_skip_post_experts_all_reduce(
441	    *,
442	    is_tp_path: bool,
443	    use_reduce_scatter: bool = False,
444	    should_allreduce_fusion: bool = False,
445	) -> bool:
446	    """Whether to skip the post-experts all-reduce (EP or TP) because a
447	    downstream component will fuse, replace, or absorb it.
448	
449	    Skip reasons, in order:
450	      - ``should_allreduce_fusion``: LayerCommunicator will fuse the all-reduce
451	        with the next layer's residual all-reduce.
452	      - ``use_reduce_scatter``: LayerCommunicator's post-attention scatter will
453	        do reduce-scatter, which would double-reduce on top of an all-reduce.
454	      - ``should_use_dp_reduce_scatterv()``: the standard dispatcher's combine
455	        path replaces the all-reduce with a reduce-scatterv.
456	      - ``should_use_flashinfer_cutlass_moe_fp4_allgather()`` (TP path only):
457	        the flashinfer cutlass FP4 kernel performs an all-gather that absorbs
458	        the post-experts TP all-reduce. Not relevant to the EP all-reduce.
459	      - ``get_moe_a2a_backend().is_flashinfer()``: the flashinfer A2A
460	        dispatcher's ``MoeAlltoAll.combine`` already alltoall-reduces partial
461	        MoE outputs back to the source rank, so any further EP/TP all-reduce
462	        would double-count and overflow BF16. Mirrors TRTLLM's
463	        ``not enable_alltoall`` gate
464	        (``tensorrt_llm/_torch/modules/fused_moe/interface.py:879``).
465	
466	    The first two args are layer-context flags from ``LayerCommunicator`` and
467	    default to ``False`` for models that don't use it. Pass ``is_tp_path=True``
468	    for the post-experts TP all-reduce, ``False`` for the EP all-reduce.
469	    """
470	    if should_allreduce_fusion or use_reduce_scatter:
471	        return True
472	    if should_use_dp_reduce_scatterv():
473	        return True
474	    if is_tp_path and should_use_flashinfer_cutlass_moe_fp4_allgather():
475	        return True
476	    if get_moe_a2a_backend().is_flashinfer():
477	        return True
478	    return False

481	@contextmanager
482	def speculative_moe_backend_context():
483	    """
484	    Context manager to temporarily use the speculative MoE backend for draft model operations.
485	    This ensures that draft models in speculative decoding use the configured speculative backend.
486	    """
487	    global MOE_RUNNER_BACKEND
488	    original_backend = MOE_RUNNER_BACKEND
489	    try:
490	        MOE_RUNNER_BACKEND = get_speculative_moe_runner_backend()
491	        yield
492	    finally:
493	        MOE_RUNNER_BACKEND = original_backend
494	
495	
496	@contextmanager
497	def speculative_moe_a2a_backend_context():
498	    """
499	    Context manager to temporarily use the speculative MoE A2A backend for draft model operations.
500	    This ensures that draft models in speculative decoding use the configured speculative A2A backend.
501	    """
502	    global MOE_A2A_BACKEND
503	    global DISABLE_FLASHINFER_CUTLASS_MOE_FP4_ALLGATHER
504	    original_backend = MOE_A2A_BACKEND
505	    original_disable_flashinfer_cutlass_moe_fp4_allgather = (
506	        DISABLE_FLASHINFER_CUTLASS_MOE_FP4_ALLGATHER
507	    )
508	    try:
509	        MOE_A2A_BACKEND = get_speculative_moe_a2a_backend()
510	        # Disable FP4 allgather for spec decode since MTP layers are unquantized
511	        DISABLE_FLASHINFER_CUTLASS_MOE_FP4_ALLGATHER = True
512	        yield
513	    finally:
514	        MOE_A2A_BACKEND = original_backend
515	        DISABLE_FLASHINFER_CUTLASS_MOE_FP4_ALLGATHER = (
516	            original_disable_flashinfer_cutlass_moe_fp4_allgather
517	        )

520	# The type of method in top-K routing, for use in torch custom op
521	# Please keep this in sync with the counterpart defined in https://github.com/flashinfer-ai/flashinfer/blob/main/include/flashinfer/trtllm/fused_moe/runner.h
522	class RoutingMethodType(IntEnum):
523	    # Default: Softmax -> TopK
524	    Default = (0,)
525	    # Renormalize: TopK -> Softmax
526	    Renormalize = (1,)
527	    # DeepSeekV3: Sigmoid -> RoutingBiasAdd -> Top2 in group -> Top4 groups -> Top8 experts from the Top4 groups
528	    DeepSeekV3 = (2,)
529	    # Llama4: Top1 -> Sigmoid
530	    Llama4 = (3,)
531	    # Qwen3: Softmax -> TopK -> Renormalize
532	    RenormalizeNaive = (4,)
533	    # TopK only (no softmax)
534	    TopK = (5,)
535	    # SigmoidRenorm: Sigmoid -> TopK -> Renormalize
536	    SigmoidRenorm = (6,)
537	    # MiniMax2
538	    MiniMax2 = (7,)
539	    # Sigmoid: Sigmoid -> TopK (no renormalize)
540	    Sigmoid = (8,)
541	    # Unspecified
542	    Unspecified = 9

545	AITER_PADDING_SIZE = 128
546	TRITON_PADDING_SIZE = 128

548	
549	# Unit of padding - context dependent
550	def get_moe_padding_size(is_aiter_moe):
551	    if is_aiter_moe:
552	        return AITER_PADDING_SIZE
553	    else:
554	        return (
555	            TRITON_PADDING_SIZE
556	            if bool(int(os.getenv("SGLANG_MOE_PADDING", "0")))
557	            else 0
558	        )

560	
561	def get_moe_weight_sizes(inter_dim, is_concat, is_packed, is_aiter_moe):
562	    """
563	    Calculate dimensions for MoE weight tensors.
564	
565	    Args:
566	        inter_dim: Base intermediate dimension.
567	        is_concat: If True, fusions W1 (gate) and W3 (up) projections.
568	        is_packed: If True, uses 4-bit quantization (two FP4 elements per byte).
569	        is_aiter_moe: If True, applies Aiter-specific kernel padding alignment.
570	    """
571	    # w2_down_dim is the packing rank, but w13_up_dim not (of matrix to matmul)
572	    w13_up_dim = 2 * inter_dim if is_concat else inter_dim
573	    w2_down_dim = inter_dim // 2 if is_packed else inter_dim
574	
575	    if is_aiter_moe:
576	        padding_size = get_moe_padding_size(True)
577	        align_aiter = lambda n: ((n + padding_size - 1) // padding_size) * padding_size
578	        is_padded = (w2_down_dim % padding_size) > 0
579	        if is_padded:
580	            # w2_down_dim, padding & aligned, unit: parameter dtype
581	            w2_down_dim = align_aiter(w2_down_dim)
582	        # up proj + gate fusion : 2x
583	        if is_concat:
584	            w13_up_dim = w2_down_dim * 2
585	        # packed
586	        if hasattr(torch, "float4_e2m1fn_x2") and is_packed:
587	            # w13_up_dim (row rank of matmul matrix) is not packing dim, *2 to recover
588	            w13_up_dim *= 2
589	
590	    return (w13_up_dim, w2_down_dim, False if not is_aiter_moe else is_padded)
591

87	def create_moe_dispatcher(moe_runner_config: MoeRunnerConfig) -> BaseDispatcher:
88	    a2a_backend = get_moe_a2a_backend()
89	    if (
90	        a2a_backend.is_none()
91	        or a2a_backend.is_megamoe()
92	        or a2a_backend.is_ascend_fuseep()
93	    ):
94	        # ascend_fuseep bypasses the dispatcher abstraction (see
95	        # forward_fuseep in hardware_backend/npu/moe/fuseep.py); a
96	        # StandardDispatcher is created but never invoked.
97	        return StandardDispatcher(moe_runner_config)
98	    elif (
99	        a2a_backend.is_deepep()
100	        or a2a_backend.is_mooncake()
101	        or a2a_backend.is_mori()
102	        or a2a_backend.is_nixl()
103	    ):
104	        return MaybeTboDeepEPDispatcher(
105	            group=(
106	                get_tp_group().device_group
107	                if not a2a_backend.is_mori()
108	                else get_tp_group()
109	            ),
110	            router_topk=moe_runner_config.top_k,
111	            permute_fusion=True,
112	            num_experts=moe_runner_config.num_experts,
113	            num_local_experts=moe_runner_config.num_local_experts,
114	            hidden_size=moe_runner_config.hidden_size,
115	            params_dtype=moe_runner_config.params_dtype,
116	            deepep_mode=get_deepep_mode(),
117	            async_finish=True,
118	            return_recv_hook=True,
119	        )
120	    elif a2a_backend.is_flashinfer():
121	        return FlashinferDispatcher(
122	            group=get_tp_group().device_group,
123	            router_topk=moe_runner_config.top_k,
124	            num_experts=moe_runner_config.num_experts,
125	            num_local_experts=moe_runner_config.num_local_experts,
126	            hidden_size=moe_runner_config.hidden_size,
127	        )
128	    else:
129	        raise NotImplementedError(f"Unsupported a2a backend: {a2a_backend}")
